issues.title: ElasticsearchVectorStoreProperties missing embeddingFieldName property mapping
issues.body:
**Bug description**
The value of `spring.ai.vectorstore.elasticsearch.embedding-field-name` is missed when configuring `ElasticsearchVectorStore` in `ElasticsearchVectorStoreAutoConfiguration`

**Environment**
Version 1.0.0

**Steps to reproduce**
1. set `spring.ai.vectorstore.elasticsearch.embedding-field-name` to any value except 'embedding'
2. run application
3. org.springframework.ai.vectorstore.elasticsearch.ElasticsearchVectorStoreOptions#embeddingFieldName is not set correctly

**Expected behavior**
org.springframework.ai.vectorstore.elasticsearch.ElasticsearchVectorStoreOptions#embeddingFieldName is set correctly to the `spring.ai.vectorstore.elasticsearch.embedding-field-name` property value.

issues.title: Is it supported to expose MCP tools by adding spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux to a legacy WebMVC app? No endpoint GET /sse error
issues.body:
**Bug description**
When adding the spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux starter to an existing Spring WebMVC application to expose MCP tools, accessing the /see or /sse endpoints results in an error. The actual exception is:
```
org.springframework.web.servlet.NoHandlerFoundException: No endpoint GET /sse.
    at org.springframework.web.servlet.DispatcherServlet.noHandlerFound(DispatcherServlet.java:1305)
    at org.springframework.web.servlet.DispatcherServlet.doDispatch(DispatcherServlet.java:1067)
    at org.springframework.web.servlet.DispatcherServlet.doService(DispatcherServlet.java:979)
    at org.springframework.web.servlet.FrameworkServlet.processRequest(FrameworkServlet.java:1014)
    at org.springframework.web.servlet.FrameworkServlet.doGet(FrameworkServlet.java:903)
    at jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:564)
    at org.springframework.web.servlet.FrameworkServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:885)
    at jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:658)
    at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.internalDoFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:195)
    at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.doFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:140)
    at org.apache.tomcat.websocket.server.WsFilter.doFilter(WsFilter.java:51)
    at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.internalDoFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:164)
    at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.doFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:140)
```
I am not sure if this is a bug or a misconfiguration on my side. Any help or guidance would be appreciated.
Is this kind of integration supported? Or is there any official recommendation for integrating MCP into an existing WebMVC application?

**Environment**
- spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux version: 1.0.0
- Spring Boot version: 3.4.5
- Java version: 17

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Add the spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux dependency to an existing Spring WebMVC application.
2. Start the project.
3. Visit /see or /sse in a browser.
4. The NoHandlerFoundException error above appears.

comments.body:
Hi @openai0229

I tried to write a normal web-mvc project https://github.com/sunyuhan1998/spring-ai-repro-demo/tree/main/mcp/webmvc-mcp-server which contains a `Controller` for testing, then I transformed it into an mcp server, In this demo I use the following dependencies: 

```
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

But that didn't reproduce your problem, the test controller and `/sse` both work fine. Can you provide a minimal project that reproduces the issue?

> 你好
> 
> 我尝试编写一个普通的 web-mvc 项目 https://github.com/sunyuhan1998/spring-ai-repro-demo/tree/main/mcp/webmvc-mcp-server 其中包含一个用于测试的内容，然后将其转换为 mcp 服务器，在这个演示中，我使用了以下依赖项：`Controller`
> 
> ```
> <dependency>
>     <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
>     <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
> </dependency>
> <dependency>
>     <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
>     <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc</artifactId>
> </dependency>
> ```
> 
> 但这并没有重现您的问题，测试控制器和两者都工作正常。您能否提供一个重现该问题的最小项目？`/sse`

I think what he meant was that after switching webMVC to webflux mode, MCP Server cannot detect the endpoint, even if the client has been configured properly. Because I have encountered the same problem before

> Hi [@openai0229](https://github.com/openai0229)
> 
> I tried to write a normal web-mvc project https://github.com/sunyuhan1998/spring-ai-repro-demo/tree/main/mcp/webmvc-mcp-server which contains a `Controller` for testing, then I transformed it into an mcp server, In this demo I use the following dependencies:
> 
> ```
> <dependency>
>     <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
>     <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
> </dependency>
> <dependency>
>     <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
>     <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc</artifactId>
> </dependency>
> ```
> 
> But that didn't reproduce your problem, the test controller and `/sse` both work fine. Can you provide a minimal project that reproduces the issue?

Thank you @sunyuhan1998  , I'll check out your code example first, then I'll give my minimal example

> Thank you [@sunyuhan1998](https://github.com/sunyuhan1998) , I'll check out your code example first, then I'll give my minimal example

Ok, since others in this issue have mentioned experiencing the same problem, I'm interested in this issue and look forward to your minimal example and further explanation of what's going on!

Hi @sunyuhan1998 I looked at your code, and then I found that yours is the mcp-server dependency of webmvc. Then I tried to use cline to link your mcp-server, and it was linked normally. Then I changed it to webflux and found that the link failed. I noticed that you are using version 1.1.0. Is this version that fixes some issues? I'm not sure if it's not possible to use the mcp-server dependency of webflux. My demo code is almost ready. I will give you the code repository address later.

>I noticed that you are using version 1.1.0. Is this version that fixes some issues?
I'm not sure if it's not possible to use the mcp-server dependency of webflux.
My demo code is almost ready. I will give you the code repository address later.

I'm not quite sure what adjustments were made to MCP Server in version 1.1.0, after all, it's still a development release.

In the MCP Server Boot Starter provided by Spring AI, there are three options depending on the protocol and request method you choose: 
1. stdio 
```
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server</artifactId>
</dependency>
```
3. webmvc + sse 
```
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc</artifactId>
</dependency>
```
5. flux + sse
```
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux</artifactId>
</dependency>
```
You can view more detailed instructions here: [mcp-server-boot-starter-docs](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/1.1-SNAPSHOT/api/mcp/mcp-server-boot-starter-docs.html)

In fact, the Server support for web-mvc and flux is implemented as part of the java-sdk for MCP: [mcp-spring](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/java-sdk/tree/main/mcp-spring), and the Spring AI starter just automatically configures them.

Looking forward to your demo!

> Looking forward to your demo!

Thank you @sunyuhan1998,  This is My Demo https://github.com/openai0229/spring-ai-mcp-server-demo.
I feel very strange, now my demo mcp server is not available at all, I did not write mcp-client myself, I used cline to link my mcp-server, I don't know if it's because I wrote the demo too hastily and missed something.

I reproduced your issue, and the current problem is that if both `spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux` and `spring-boot-starter-web` are used of MCP Server will make `/sse` inaccessible, but I found a way to make it usable: add the following configuration on your MCP Server side: 

```
spring:
  main:
    web-application-type: reactive
```

But I think this will bring some side effects, and I recommend you to prioritize the use of the `spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webmvc`. I'm still analyzing the cause of the problem and if it's an issue with Spring AI itself I'll try to push a PR to fix it, thanks for the report!

Thank you @sunyuhan1998 , I now found a more strange problem. After I switched webflux back to webmvc, the `mcpSyncServer` bean in the automatic configuration class `McpServerAutoConfiguration` was not loaded normally. I checked my spring startup log and found that there was no such log line. Then I tried to getBean manually after spring started, and this line of log was output normally. After that, I tested again, and my mcp service could work normally

> I reproduced your issue, and the current problem is that if both `spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux` and `spring-boot-starter-web` are used of MCP Server will make `/sse` inaccessible, but I found a way to make it usable: add the following configuration on your MCP Server side:
> 
> ```
> spring:
>   main:
>     web-application-type: reactive
> ```
> 

I checked the source code, and the issue is actually SpringBoot's default behavior: if you introduce both WebMvc and WebFlux in your project, SpringBoot will prioritize WebMvc, which is reflected in SpringBoot's code: 

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/0daf3f5897a9bad169ad7ebad6d79e4260a24822/spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/WebApplicationType.java#L60-L71


This causes endpoints such as /sse registered via `spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux` registered endpoints such as `/sse` are invalidated, I think this is a usage issue and not a problem with Spring AI itself, just that we need to improve the Spring AI documentation a bit more.

> This causes endpoints such as /sse registered via `spring-ai-starter-mcp-server-webflux` registered endpoints such as `/sse` are invalidated, I think this is a usage issue and not a problem with Spring AI itself, just that we need to improve the Spring AI documentation a bit more.

Thank you for your answer!

issues.title: Disable thinking mode for models used through Ollama like qwen3:0.6b
issues.body:
Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, the feature you are about to request might have already been requested.

**Expected Behavior**

<!--- Tell us how it should work. Add a code example to explain what you think the feature should look like. This is optional, but it would help up understand your expectations. -->

The ollama team has added thinking support to the cli and the api. 
See: https://github.com/ollama/ollama/pull/10584/ 

This parameter allows to control the output of the "thinking" process of the model 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/44342151-154e-400a-90ce-4d696d24f277)

**Current Behavior**

<!--- Explain the difference from current behavior and why do you need this feature (aka why it is not possible to implement the desired functionality with the current version) -->

At this moment, there is no way to manage the thinking parameter from the Spring AI Ollama API. 

**Context**

Adding this, will give us the ability to use thinking models, and get just the "output" without the thinking phase of the model. 

<!--- 
How has this issue affected you?
What are you trying to accomplish?
What other alternatives have you considered?
Are you aware of any workarounds?
-->

comments.body:
Hi team - I am looking forward on contributing the changes for making this possible, I do appreciate any help with the onboarding process for me, to be able to contribute to the project. 

Regards,
Hi @BrayanMnz , Thanks for the report!I've pushed a PR: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3386 before this to add that support, and it's currently sitting in the 1.1.X milestone

That's great, the changes LGTM @sunyuhan1998 
Hopefully they will be integrated soon in the project. 

This https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3386 address my concern, closing this.
